name: Åland Islands (Landskapet Åland / Ahvenanmaan maakunta)
summary: Åland Islands is a Northern European civil-law autonomous Swedish-speaking region of Finland — structurally distinctive globally as the only autonomous region with constitutionally-protected single-official-language status (Swedish-only) within a bilingual state (Finland, Finnish + Swedish), plus distinctive Åland-specific citizenship (hembygdsrätt) required for property ownership and certain other rights. Established under the 1921 League of Nations decision following the Åland Crisis, Åland's autonomy framework operates under the Act on the Autonomy of Åland 1991 (Självstyrelselag för Åland) and the demilitarisation framework under the 1856 Convention on the Demilitarisation of the Åland Islands and the 1921 Åland Convention. Family-law framework operates under Finnish family-law principles (Marriage Act 234/1929, Act on Child Custody and Right of Access 361/1983) with Åland-specific implementation. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by Finnish family-law adapted to Åland framework. The Court of Appeal of Turku (Åbo hovrätt) has jurisdiction over Åland matters; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Finnish Supreme Court. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the District Court of Åland. Psychology profession is regulated through the Finnish Healthcare Authority (Valvira) framework as applicable in Åland. Åland is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the Finnish best-interests-of-the-child standard. Åland is a Hague Convention 1980 party via Finnish territorial extension effective 1 August 1994.

statutory_framework.citation: Act on the Autonomy of Åland 1991 (Självstyrelselag för Åland)
statutory_framework.title: Act on the Autonomy of Åland
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Act establishing Åland's autonomous-region framework with legislative competence over substantial subject-matters including family-law implementation.

statutory_framework.citation: 1921 League of Nations decision + Åland Convention
statutory_framework.title: Åland Treaty
statutory_framework.relevance: 1921 League of Nations decision following Åland Crisis establishing Åland's autonomy framework under Finnish sovereignty + demilitarisation.

statutory_framework.citation: 1856 Convention on the Demilitarisation of the Åland Islands
statutory_framework.title: Convention on the Demilitarisation of the Åland Islands
statutory_framework.relevance: International Convention establishing Åland's demilitarisation framework.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Turku (Åbo hovrätt) — Åland jurisdiction
apex_courts.role: Apex appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Åland.

apex_courts.name: Finnish Supreme Court (Korkein oikeus / Högsta domstolen)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Åland.

professional_regulators.name: Finnish Healthcare Authority (Valvira)
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology as applicable in Åland.

anonymisation_convention: Åland family-court decisions are anonymised per Finnish judicial practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Fredrikshamn + Russian-Grand-Duchy-of-Finland framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of Fredrikshamn 17 September 1809 ending Finnish War — Sweden ceded Finland (including Åland) to Russian Empire — substantively distinctive Northern European Russian-Grand-Duchy-of-Finland framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and Swedish-cultural-heritage-preservation.

key_developments.title: Convention on the Demilitarisation of the Åland Islands + post-Crimean-War framework
key_developments.description: Convention on the Demilitarisation of the Åland Islands 30 March 1856 — substantively distinctive globally Anglo-French-Russian demilitarisation framework following Crimean War. Foundational substantive 170+ year demilitarisation framework persisting through subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Finnish independence + Åland-Crisis-onset + Swedish-association-attempt framework
key_developments.description: Finnish independence 6 December 1917 from Russian Empire substantively triggering Åland Crisis — substantively distinctive Northern European Åland-attempt-to-rejoin-Sweden framework. 1919 Åland petition signed by 96% of Åland adults requesting Swedish sovereignty. Foundational pre-1921-League-of-Nations-decision framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: League of Nations decision + Åland Convention + autonomy framework
key_developments.description: League of Nations Council decision 24 June 1921 following Åland Crisis establishing Åland's autonomy framework under Finnish sovereignty — substantively distinctive globally first-League-of-Nations-international-minority-protection-decision framework. Åland Convention 20 October 1921 reaffirming demilitarisation framework. Substantively significant 100+ year internationally-guaranteed-autonomy framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Finnish Marriage Act 234/1929 + Åland-applicable framework
key_developments.description: Finnish Marriage Act 234/1929 applicable to Åland establishing substantive matrimonial-law framework — substantively significant Northern European Finnish-family-law-Åland-applicable framework. Foundational substantive matrimonial-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Finnish Act on Child Custody and Right of Access 361/1983 + Åland-applicable framework
key_developments.description: Finnish Act on Child Custody and Right of Access 361/1983 applicable to Åland establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive framework — substantively significant Northern European Finnish-child-custody-Åland-applicable framework. Foundational substantive child-custody framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Act on the Autonomy of Åland (Självstyrelselag för Åland) + modern-autonomy framework
key_developments.description: Act on the Autonomy of Åland 1991 (Självstyrelselag för Åland) adopted establishing Åland's modern autonomous-region framework with legislative competence over substantial subject-matters including family-law implementation. Substantively significant Northern European autonomous-region-legislative-competence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension + Finland-UNCRC-Åland-extension framework
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by Finland to Åland effective 1 August 1994. Finland ratified UNCRC 20 June 1991 with extension to Åland — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Finnish-derivative-civil-law framework applicable to Åland.

key_developments.title: Åland EU-membership-Protocol-2 + special-status framework
key_developments.description: Åland Protocol 2 to Finland's EU accession Treaty 1994 (effective 1995) — substantively distinctive globally EU-membership-with-Åland-special-status framework. Åland retains hembygdsrätt citizenship requirements + restricted right-of-establishment + limited acquis communautaire applicability. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Sjögren premiership + Lagting framework + Hembygdsrätt framework consolidation
key_developments.description: Katrin Sjögren (Liberalerna) premiership 2019-2023 framework consolidation. Veronica Thörnroos (Åländsk Center) subsequent premiership framework. Lagting (Åland parliament) framework substantively significant institutional context. Hembygdsrätt (Åland citizenship) framework substantively significant institutional context affecting cross-border family-law adjudication. Substantively significant Nordic autonomous-territory institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Åbo hovrätt + Finnish Supreme Court — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + continuing-autonomy-framework
key_developments.description: Court of Appeal of Turku (Åbo hovrätt) and Finnish Supreme Court (Korkein oikeus / Högsta domstolen) continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Finnish Marriage Act 234/1929 + Act on Child Custody 361/1983 + Act on Autonomy of Åland 1991 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within continuing Åland autonomy framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Åland Islands is structurally distinctive globally within the corpus as the only autonomous region with constitutionally-protected single-official-language status (Swedish-only) within a bilingual state (Finland).

Åland-specific citizenship (hembygdsrätt) required for property ownership and certain other rights is structurally distinctive globally — only state-level entity with sub-state-level citizenship distinction in the corpus.
1921 League of Nations-era origin of autonomy framework is structurally distinctive — among the oldest internationally-guaranteed autonomy frameworks in the corpus.
Demilitarisation under 1856 Convention + 1921 Åland Convention places Åland alongside Svalbard in the demilitarised-territory cluster.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via Finnish territorial extension reflects autonomous-region Hague jurisdiction status.
editorial_notes: Åland Islands jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Treaty-of-Fredrikshamn-1809-to-continuing-autonomy-framework trajectory: 1809-Treaty-of-Fredrikshamn-+-Russian-Grand-Duchy-of-Finland + 1856-Convention-on-Demilitarisation + 1917-Finnish-independence-+-Åland-Crisis-+-Swedish-association-attempt + 1921-League-of-Nations-decision-+-Åland-Convention + 1929-Finnish-Marriage-Act-Åland-applicable + 1983-Finnish-Act-on-Child-Custody-Åland-applicable + 1991-Act-on-Autonomy-of-Åland-Självstyrelselag + 1994-Hague-Convention-1980-+-UNCRC-Åland-extension + 1995-Åland-EU-Protocol-2 + 2024-Åbo-hovrätt-+-Finnish-Supreme-Court-best-interests.

Civil-law Northern European autonomous Swedish-speaking region of Finland (Act on the Autonomy of Åland 1991 + 1921 League of Nations decision + 1856 demilitarisation Convention + 1995 EU Protocol 2 + constitutionally-protected Swedish-only official language + hembygdsrätt citizenship + Hague via Finnish territorial extension 1994). Only single-official-language autonomous region within bilingual state globally + only sub-state-level citizenship distinction globally + first League of Nations international minority protection decision (1921).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Finnish Marriage Act 234/1929 + Act on Child Custody 361/1983 + Act on Autonomy of Åland 1991 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Northern-European + civil-law + Finnish-autonomous-region-cluster + constitutionally-protected-Swedish-only-language-within-bilingual-Finnish-state-globally-distinctive + hembygdsrätt-sub-state-citizenship-globally-distinctive + first-League-of-Nations-international-minority-protection-decision-1921-globally-distinctive + 170-year-demilitarisation-since-1856 + Treaty-of-Fredrikshamn-1809-Russian-Grand-Duchy + Åland-Crisis-1917-1921-Swedish-association-attempt + Åland-Protocol-2-EU-special-status-1995 + Hague-via-Finnish-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
